message:
feat(mvcc,journal): chain-cap enforcement after COW writes, journal apply/clear, and compression dedup improvements

MVCC store changes:
- Move chain_cap enforcement after COW intent writes so physical
  versions are also capped, preventing unbounded version accumulation
  on blocks that receive both logical and physical writes in the same
  transaction.
- Replace fragile std::ptr::eq version lookup with enumerate-based
  index in compression_stats(), eliminating potential UB on pointer
  comparison across non-contiguous allocations.
- Refactor maybe_dedup to use versions.is_empty() guard instead of
  pattern matching on last(), simplifying the dedup entry path.
- Add crossbeam-epoch dependency for concurrent GC in sharded store.

Journal changes:
- Add CommittedTxn type alias for clarity in replay pipelines.
- Implement apply_replay(): write-back committed transaction blocks
  to device with per-block verification read-back to detect silent
  corruption during journal application.
- Implement clear_journal(): zero all blocks in a JBD2 journal region
  so subsequent mounts skip replay (safe to interrupt; incomplete
  clear simply triggers idempotent replay on next mount).

Compression:
- Tighten resolve_data_with call sites to pass enumerated index
  directly instead of searching by pointer identity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
